title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy ears
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy ears are natural remedies made from a variety of herbs and plants that can help alleviate the discomfort of itchy ears. These teas typically contain ingredients like chamomile, calendula, and mullein, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce itching and irritation in the ears. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from itchy ears and improve their overall quality of life. For example, a person who frequently experiences itchy ears due to allergies or ear infections can find relief by drinking herbal teas containing calendula and chamomile. These teas can help reduce inflammation and soothe the ears, providing much-needed relief and allowing individuals to go about their daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with itchy ears because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated skin and reduce itching. The tea's antimicrobial agents also combat bacterial, fungal, or viral infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, yarrow's natural astringent qualities help to dry up excessive discharge and wax buildup, which can contribute to ear itchiness. As a result, drinking herbal yarrow teas can provide relief from the discomfort and annoyance of itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic properties: Yarrow tea's antiseptic properties help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing itchiness in the ear canal.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in yarrow tea, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can soothe and calm irritated skin in the ear canal, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: Yarrow tea's ability to stimulate blood flow and promote wound healing can help to repair damaged or irritated tissues in the ear canal, reducing itching and promoting healthy tissue growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated ear tissues.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, they provide natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties to combat infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities for the desired amount of tea.
Rinse the yarrow plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried yarrow or three teaspoons of fresh yarrow with eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes before straining and serving.
Enjoy the tea hot or iced, sweetened with honey if desired, within a few hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to reduce swelling and itching, providing relief from the discomfort of otitis externa. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause ear irritation, promoting a healthy and comfortable ear canal.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Pot marigold's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy ears, promoting a soothing and calming effect.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties help combat infections and inflammation caused by bacterial or fungal overgrowth in the ear canal, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.

Wound-healing and skin-soothing compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations, which can help alleviate itchy ears caused by minor traumas or allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial properties, roots help soothe and calm itchiness caused by fungal or bacterial infections.
Flowers: Packed with anti-inflammatory compounds, flowers reduce swelling and redness associated with ear irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom, carefully picking only the bright yellow petals.
Rinse the collected calendula flowers gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh calendula flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the measured calendula flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Allow the infused tea to cool slightly before serving hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work to calm irritated skin and reduce swelling, providing fast relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of chamomile help to combat infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. By drinking the tea or applying it topically in a cooled-down form, individuals can find quick and natural relief from itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and redness in the ear canal, providing relief from itchy and irritated ears.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of German Chamomile can help combat infections and eliminate bacteria that may be causing ear itching, discomfort, and irritation.
Soothing and calming: German Chamomile's calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress-related ear tension, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort in the ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of German chamomile contain a high concentration of apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated ear tissue.
Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in azulene, a gentle and effective antiseptic that reduces inflammation and promotes healing in itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every eight ounces water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan or kettle.
Remove the boiling water from heat and add the measured dried chamomilla flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes then strain the liquid into a teapot.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 15-20 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as echinacea and quercetin, soothe irritated skin and reduce swelling, providing quick relief from the discomfort associated with itchy ears. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial agents help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the itchiness. By drinking eastern purple coneflower teas regularly, individuals can find natural and effective relief from itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenoids, help reduce swelling and alleviate itching in the ear canal.
Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial agents, including luteolin and quercetin, combat bacterial infections that can cause ear itchiness and discomfort.
Anti-fungal properties: The tea's antifungal compounds, such as ursolic acid, help prevent fungal overgrowth in the ear canal, which can contribute to itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are often used to soothe itching and reduce inflammation associated with ear infections.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and flavonoids, seeds have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections causing itchiness in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves at peak blooming season or store dried forms from reputable sources.
Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water and add to a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired tea strength and flavor profile, before straining with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, as desired, and stir gently to combine with the infused echinacea purpurea liquid.
Pour the prepared tea into cups and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation, refrigerating any leftovers for up to three days if needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the skin in the ear canal. The tea's antibacterial compounds also help to reduce any underlying infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum has natural antiseptic properties that promote a healthy environment within the ear, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive wax buildup or other debris.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eucalyptus oil, found in Tasmanian Blue Gum, contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which may help reduce swelling and itching associated with itchy ears.

Antibacterial properties: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as cineole and alpha-terpinene, exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial strains, including those that can cause ear infections, potentially helping to alleviate itchy ears.
Decongestant and expectorant effects: Eucalyptus oil can help loosen and clear excess mucus from the ears, which may provide relief from itchiness caused by ear congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce swelling and itching in the ears.
Bark: The bark contains natural astringent properties that help dry up excess discharge and soothe irritated ear tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning for optimal oil content retention.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade or sun drying methods within 24 hours of harvesting.
Measure one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and add to a boiling water kettle filled with 8 ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve hot or allow to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alder buckthorn teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's natural astringent qualities also help to reduce excessive wax production in the ear canal, relieving itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, alder buckthorn contains antioxidants that promote healthy ear tissue and may even help to prevent infections that can cause itchy ears. As a result, drinking alder buckthorn teas can provide natural relief from itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's flavonoids and tannins help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear canal, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by conditions like otitis externa.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's triterpenoids and polyphenols exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria, fungi, and viruses that can cause infections leading to itchy ears, such as middle ear infections (otitis media).

Astringent and antipruritic properties: The tea's tannins and flavonoids have an astringent effect on the skin, helping to dry up excessive discharge and reduce itching caused by conditions like eczema, psoriasis, or contact dermatitis affecting the ear canal.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing itching and discomfort in the ears.
Bark: Containing a high amount of tannins, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried frangula alnus leaves and stems in late summer or early fall.
Rinse the collected herb material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried alder buckthorn herb per 8 oz cup boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup to remove solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and allow it to cool to room temperature within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The herbal tea contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces swelling and redness in the ear canal, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, licorice teas' natural antibacterial properties help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants causing the itchiness, promoting a healthy and comfortable ear environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glycyrrhetinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal and alleviate itching.
Antibacterial properties: Licorice tea has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against various types of bacteria, including those that can cause ear infections and contribute to itchy ears.

Sothing and protective properties: The saponins present in licorice root have been found to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the ear canal, providing relief from itching and discomfort while also helping to protect the ear canal from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with ear infections.
Bark: Contains compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots per cup of boiling water.
Steep the licorice root mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste if desired before serving hot or chilled.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When brewed into a tea, the herb's active compounds work to reduce redness and swelling in the ear canal, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, st john's wort's natural antiseptic properties help to combat any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to the itchiness, promoting a healthier and more comfortable ear environment.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal compounds: These properties can help combat bacterial or fungal infections in the ear canal, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with itchy ears.
Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and alleviate itching caused by ear infections or allergies.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort tea's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, further reducing itchiness and discomfort in the ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm itchy ears.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and hypericin, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help to combat infections and reduce itching in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and measure out 2 teaspoons per cup.
Heat fresh filtered water to a rolling boil in a saucepan or electric kettle.
Steep the dried st. john's wort mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoy within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with itchy ears because its calming properties soothe irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The antiseptic qualities of lavender also help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds can ease swelling and redness in the ear canal, providing relief from the itching and discomfort associated with itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: English lavender tea has antibacterial properties that can help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the ear canal, which may contribute to itchiness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in English lavender tea can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Calming and soothing properties: English lavender tea has a calming and soothing effect on the body, which can help to relax tense muscles and nerves in the ears, reducing itchiness and promoting overall comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, lavender roots help soothe itchiness and inflammation in the ear canal.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lavender contain antiseptic properties that calm irritation and reduce discomfort in itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried english lavender flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the lavender mixture for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Combine the lavender tea with honey to taste and stir until dissolved.
Serve the english lavender tea hot or chilled within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with itchy ears because its calming properties soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and itching. The antiviral and antibacterial compounds in lemon balm tea also help to combat infections that can cause ear itching. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory effects reduce redness and swelling, providing instant relief from uncomfortable ear irritation. Drinking lemon balm tea or applying a cooled tea compress to the affected area can provide quick and effective relief from itchy ears.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: Lemon balm tea helps alleviate itching and inflammation in the ears by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial compounds present in lemon balm tea can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to itchy ears, reducing symptoms such as itchiness, redness, and discharge.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm irritated ear tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe itchy ears by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in vitamin C and flavonoids, which have antiseptic properties that help combat infections causing itchiness in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves for each eight ounce cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 200-212°f and pour it over the herb in a tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the herbal mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the flavors and active compounds to release.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or strainer and discard the solids after pouring.
Add one to two tablespoons of honey or sugar to taste if desired, then enjoy the warm and soothing brew.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy ears with herbal teas is to steep dried herbs in hot water to release their active compounds. Chamomile, calendula, and lavender are all excellent choices due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Once the tea has cooled, use a clean dropper to apply a few drops of the liquid into the affected ear, taking care to keep the liquid away from the ear canal. Allow the tea to sit in the ear for several minutes before draining. Repeat this process several times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy ears that are treated with herbal teas include ear infections and inflammation. Herbal teas with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, such as chamomile and echinacea, can help alleviate these symptoms. Chamomile tea has soothing effects that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the ear canal, while echinacea tea can boost the immune system and fight off infections. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment, especially if symptoms persist or worsen.
